headline: OIL BLOCKADE: U.S. Seals Iran Ports, Crude Hits $103 — Rate Hike Risk Now Threatens Metal Rally
snippet: The U.S. military's enforcement of an Iranian port blockade on April 13 sent Brent crude surging 7% to $103/barrel, while U.S. CPI printed at 3.3% — the highest since May 2024 — prompting the ECB and Bank of England to signal potential June rate hikes. Gold and silver are seeing outflows as investors weigh whether oil-driven inflation will force a central bank pivot that undercuts the precious metals bull case.

headline: ANTIMONY IN NEVADA: NevGold Hits 0.34% Sb and 0.41 g/t Au on Historic Leach Pads, MRE Due Q2 2026
snippet: NevGold Corp. reported results from the first 10 of 17 sonic drill holes at its Limousine Butte Project in Nevada, returning up to 0.34% antimony and 0.55 g/t gold over 15.5m from historic gold leach pads. A maiden antimony-gold Mineral Resource Estimate is targeted for Q2 2026, with near-term antimony production from the pads potentially achievable by 2027 — putting a U.S.-hosted antimony source within reach at a time China controls global supply.

headline: CHINA RARE EARTH INDEX HITS 267 — Q2 Concentrate Prices Jump 44.6% as Beijing Tightens Supply Grip
snippet: China's domestic rare earth price index printed 267.0 on April 13, while China Northern Rare Earth set its Q2 2026 concentrate transaction price at 38,804 yuan/tonne — a 44.6% jump over Q1 — revealing upstream tightness even as some export controls remain suspended until November 2026. The data reinforces how Beijing continues to dictate rare earth pricing globally while maintaining the leverage to reimpose restrictions at will.

headline: INDONESIA HIKES NICKEL ORE PRICE — New Formula Takes Effect April 15 as LME Nickel Surges 2.6%
snippet: Indonesia's Ministry of Energy and Mineral Resources revised its nickel ore benchmark pricing formula on April 13, raising the correction factor for 1.6%-grade ore from 17% to 30% and adding cobalt byproduct credits — with the new formula effective April 15. LME nickel futures jumped 2.6% to a one-month high on the news, squeezing Indonesian processors already facing pressure from Middle East conflict driving up energy costs.

headline: K92 MINING Q1: 46,743 oz AuEq at Kainantu, 37% Production Jump as Stage 3 Expansion Hits 96% Committed
snippet: K92 Mining's Kainantu gold mine in Papua New Guinea delivered 46,743 oz AuEq in Q1 2026, with tonnes processed up 37% year-over-year and a new monthly development record of 1,067m achieved in March. The Stage 3 Expansion is 96% spent or committed and on budget, keeping full-year guidance of 190,000–225,000 oz AuEq firmly intact.

headline: McFarlane Lake Mining Drills 136m at 0.67 g/t Gold at Juby's Golden Lake Deposit, Ontario
snippet: McFarlane Lake Mining reported drill hole GL 26-73A at the Golden Lake deposit on its Juby Gold Project in Ontario, intersecting 136 metres grading 0.67 g/t gold — a broad, potentially bulk-mineable intercept demonstrating strong deposit continuity. The Juby Project sits in a district attracting growing attention from junior gold operators, with further assay results from the ongoing program expected in coming weeks.

headline: Arbor Metals Wraps Phase 1 Drilling at Jarnet Lithium, Adjacent to Patriot Battery Metals' Quebec Discovery
snippet: Arbor Metals completed its Phase 1 2026 drill program at the Jarnet Lithium Project in James Bay, Quebec, drilling 5 holes totaling 1,433 metres directly adjacent to Patriot Battery Metals' Shaakichiuwaanaan lithium property. Assay results are pending for what management describes as one of Quebec's most strategically sited lithium exploration properties, with proximity to a major discovery a key value driver.

headline: Bonterra Resources Launches 12,000m Drill Campaign at Desmaraisville South, Near Quebec's Bachelor Mill
snippet: Bonterra Resources launched a 10,000–12,000m diamond drill program at its Desmaraisville South Project in Quebec, targeting deep structures adjacent to the O'Brien intrusive that hosts the Bachelor and Moroy gold deposits. The Bachelor Mill is the only fully permitted processing facility in the region, giving Bonterra a critical infrastructure edge as results come in across this year's program.

headline: DEEP-SEA MILLIONS: Will TMC The Metals Company's Pacific Nodules Reward Investors in 12 Months?
snippet: The Motley Fool examines whether TMC The Metals Company — targeting polymetallic nodules on the Pacific seafloor rich in nickel, cobalt, copper, and manganese — could reward investors as the Trump administration accelerates its NOAA permitting review. Supporters see a potential low-cost critical mineral source outside Chinese supply chains; skeptics flag regulatory and environmental uncertainty as the key risk before any commercial production begins.
